feedback.text: If I buy a digital product, such as a video game, then I should actually get the game for my money, not just a time-limited licence, as more and more providers now write in their EULAs. Such licences, which can be revoked at any time and for whatever reason, not only reduce the end-user’s trust in the company behind it, but also limit the preservation of video games as a whole. And if a digital store mentions ‘purchase’, then I also expect to actually own the purchased product and not just obtain a licence to use it.
